entrance (P9721) == * Katsuraginimasuhonoikazuchi Jinja Torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Katsuragi-imasuhonoikaduchi-jinja}} [[JA:葛木坐火雷神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11620954}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine |画像=[[File:Fuefuki-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |所在地={{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Katsuragi|Q659672}}Fuefuki 448 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|Honoikazuchi Ōkami|Q10892534}}
{{jalink|Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849}} |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin major}}, 2 seats)
former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建=Unknown (reign of {{jalink|Emperor Jinmu|Q200188}}) |本殿={{jalink|Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545}} |別名=Fuefuki Shrine |例祭={{jalink|October 25|Q2954}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Katsuraki ni Imasu
Honoikaduchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Fuefuki-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine''' (かつらきにいますほのいかづちじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Fuefuki, {{jalink|Katsuragi|Q659672}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and under the former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} system it was a {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. It is commonly called "Fuefuki Shrine." === Enshrined deities === Its principal deities are {{jalink|Honoikazuchi Ōkami|Q10892534}} and {{jalink|Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849}}, with {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Takamimusubi||Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ninigi|Q1056017|Amatsu-hiko Hono-ninigi-no-Mikoto}}, and Ikohitsuhe-no-Mikoto additionally enshrined. The original deity of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine was Honoikazuchi Ōkami, while Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto was the deity of Fuefuki Shrine. Honoikazuchi Ōkami is also called a thunder deity, but here is worshipped as a fire deity. === History === === Overview === The date of foundation is unknown; shrine tradition says either in the age of the gods or in the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Jinmu|Q200188}}. The first documentary appearance is in the ''Buntoku Tennō Jitsuroku'', where in {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} 2 (852), fourth month, it was granted the divine rank of {{jalink|Senior Third Rank|Q11123261}}. According to the {{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}, on the 27th day of the first month of {{jalink|Jōgan|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 1 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), it was granted Junior Second Rank. In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} it is listed as "two seats of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine in Oshiami District, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}," ranked as myojin taisha and receiving offerings for monthly, aidame, and niiname rites. After this, however, references to the shrine disappear. Shrine tradition says the shrine declined in the Heian period and became a {{jalink|subsidiary shrine|Q135009906}} of Fuefuki Shrine in this area. Fuefuki Shrine is thought to have been founded by the Fuefuki no Muraji clan, who were based here. Its deity Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto is their ancestral deity. Behind the honden is an ancient tomb said to be that of {{jalink|Taketahori-no-Mikoto|Q97706292|Taketawake-no-Mikoto}}, father of Kaiko, ancestor of the Fuefuki no Muraji. In {{jalink|1874|Q7761}} (Meiji 7), Honoikazuchi-sha, then a sub-shrine of Fuefuki Shrine, was merged into Fuefuki Shrine, the shrine was renamed Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine, and ranked as gōsha. === Divine rank === * {{jalink|Ten'an|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}) {{jalink|January 27|Q2284}}: from Senior Third Rank, Second Merit Rank to Junior Second Rank, Second Merit Rank ({{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}) - written as "Katsuraki Honoikazuchi deity." === Precincts === There is a Russian-made cannon in the precincts, dedicated by the government after the {{jalink|Russo-Japanese War|Q159950}}. Fuefuki-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden Fuefukijinja Kofun, funkyu.jpg|{{jalink|Fuefuki Shrine Kofun|Q124707325}} behind the honden Fuefuki-jinja, sandou.jpg|Approach in front of haiden Fuefuki-jinja, keidai.jpg|Precincts Fuefuki-jinja, taihou.jpg|Dedicated cannon in the precincts === Rituals === * Otaue Festival ** {{jalink|February 11|Q2335}} ** Early spring festival announcing spring's arrival in Katsuragi. ** After Kinen-sai is held in the haiden, the Otaue rite is performed in the broad court, including mimed rice-planting actions. Then rice cakes are thrown. * Nagoshi Festival ** {{jalink|July 17|Q2729}} ** Parishioners carrying twelve lantern poles visit while singing and accompanying Ise-ondo. The rows of lanterns in the broad court create a fantastic scene. ** Includes dedication performance by the "Katsuragi Taiko Preservation Society." * Chinka Festival ** {{jalink|November 15|Q2982}} ** Following old rites, fire is kindled by friction, giving thanks to fire and praying for fire safety. === Cultural properties === === Nara Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Historic site ** Fuefuki Shrine Kofun - designated March 20, 1998 (Heisei 10). * Natural monument ** Ichiigashi grove of Fuefuki Shrine - designated March 10, 1997 (listed as Shōwa 62 in source). === Access === * 30 minutes west on foot from {{jalink|Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085}} {{jalink|Gose Line}} {{jalink|Oshimi Station|Q7106624}}. === Related items === * {{jalink|Fuefuki Shrine Kofun|Q124707325}} * {{jalink|Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba|Q24862683}} - among fans, this shrine is regarded as one of the pilgrimage "holy sites"[https://jocr.jp/raditopi/2020/08/26/123445/ Is the enshrined deity's name the source of a powerful move name in "Demon Slayer"?! - Interview with the handsome chief priest of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine, Radio Kansai Topics]. === Sources === {{reflist}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Katsuragi-imasuhonoikaduchi-jinja}} *[http://fuefukijinja.org Fuefuki Shrine top page] (official site) *[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22301 Two seats of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto/Shrine Historical Sources" * {{Twitter|fuefuki85}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらきいますほのいかつちしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:gōshi]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:葛城市の歴史]] [[Category:葛城市の建築物]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|名かつらきいますほのいかつちしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

entrance (P9721) == * Katsuraginimasuhonoikazuchi Jinja Torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Katsuragi-imasuhonoikaduchi-jinja}} [[JA:葛木坐火雷神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11620954}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine |画像=[[File:Fuefuki-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |所在地={{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Katsuragi|Q659672}}Fuefuki 448 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|Honoikazuchi Ōkami|Q10892534}}
{{jalink|Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849}} |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin major}}, 2 seats)
former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建=Unknown (reign of {{jalink|Emperor Jinmu|Q200188}}) |本殿={{jalink|Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545}} |別名=Fuefuki Shrine |例祭={{jalink|October 25|Q2954}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Katsuraki ni Imasu
Honoikaduchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Fuefuki-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine''' (かつらきにいますほのいかづちじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Fuefuki, {{jalink|Katsuragi|Q659672}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and under the former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} system it was a {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. It is commonly called "Fuefuki Shrine." === Enshrined deities === Its principal deities are {{jalink|Honoikazuchi Ōkami|Q10892534}} and {{jalink|Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849}}, with {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Takamimusubi||Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ninigi|Q1056017|Amatsu-hiko Hono-ninigi-no-Mikoto}}, and Ikohitsuhe-no-Mikoto additionally enshrined. The original deity of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine was Honoikazuchi Ōkami, while Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto was the deity of Fuefuki Shrine. Honoikazuchi Ōkami is also called a thunder deity, but here is worshipped as a fire deity. === History === === Overview === The date of foundation is unknown; shrine tradition says either in the age of the gods or in the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Jinmu|Q200188}}. The first documentary appearance is in the ''Buntoku Tennō Jitsuroku'', where in {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} 2 (852), fourth month, it was granted the divine rank of {{jalink|Senior Third Rank|Q11123261}}. According to the {{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}, on the 27th day of the first month of {{jalink|Jōgan|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 1 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), it was granted Junior Second Rank. In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} it is listed as "two seats of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine in Oshiami District, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}," ranked as myojin taisha and receiving offerings for monthly, aidame, and niiname rites. After this, however, references to the shrine disappear. Shrine tradition says the shrine declined in the Heian period and became a {{jalink|subsidiary shrine|Q135009906}} of Fuefuki Shrine in this area. Fuefuki Shrine is thought to have been founded by the Fuefuki no Muraji clan, who were based here. Its deity Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto is their ancestral deity. Behind the honden is an ancient tomb said to be that of {{jalink|Taketahori-no-Mikoto|Q97706292|Taketawake-no-Mikoto}}, father of Kaiko, ancestor of the Fuefuki no Muraji. In {{jalink|1874|Q7761}} (Meiji 7), Honoikazuchi-sha, then a sub-shrine of Fuefuki Shrine, was merged into Fuefuki Shrine, the shrine was renamed Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine, and ranked as gōsha. === Divine rank === * {{jalink|Ten'an|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}) {{jalink|January 27|Q2284}}: from Senior Third Rank, Second Merit Rank to Junior Second Rank, Second Merit Rank ({{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}) - written as "Katsuraki Honoikazuchi deity." === Precincts === There is a Russian-made cannon in the precincts, dedicated by the government after the {{jalink|Russo-Japanese War|Q159950}}. Fuefuki-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden Fuefukijinja Kofun, funkyu.jpg|{{jalink|Fuefuki Shrine Kofun|Q124707325}} behind the honden Fuefuki-jinja, sandou.jpg|Approach in front of haiden Fuefuki-jinja, keidai.jpg|Precincts Fuefuki-jinja, taihou.jpg|Dedicated cannon in the precincts === Rituals === * Otaue Festival ** {{jalink|February 11|Q2335}} ** Early spring festival announcing spring's arrival in Katsuragi. ** After Kinen-sai is held in the haiden, the Otaue rite is performed in the broad court, including mimed rice-planting actions. Then rice cakes are thrown. * Nagoshi Festival ** {{jalink|July 17|Q2729}} ** Parishioners carrying twelve lantern poles visit while singing and accompanying Ise-ondo. The rows of lanterns in the broad court create a fantastic scene. ** Includes dedication performance by the "Katsuragi Taiko Preservation Society." * Chinka Festival ** {{jalink|November 15|Q2982}} ** Following old rites, fire is kindled by friction, giving thanks to fire and praying for fire safety. === Cultural properties === === Nara Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Historic site ** Fuefuki Shrine Kofun - designated March 20, 1998 (Heisei 10). * Natural monument ** Ichiigashi grove of Fuefuki Shrine - designated March 10, 1997 (listed as Shōwa 62 in source). === Access === * 30 minutes west on foot from {{jalink|Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085}} {{jalink|Gose Line}} {{jalink|Oshimi Station|Q7106624}}. === Related items === * {{jalink|Fuefuki Shrine Kofun|Q124707325}} * {{jalink|Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba|Q24862683}} - among fans, this shrine is regarded as one of the pilgrimage "holy sites"[https://jocr.jp/raditopi/2020/08/26/123445/ Is the enshrined deity's name the source of a powerful move name in "Demon Slayer"?! - Interview with the handsome chief priest of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine, Radio Kansai Topics]. === Sources === {{reflist}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Katsuragi-imasuhonoikaduchi-jinja}} *[http://fuefukijinja.org Fuefuki Shrine top page] (official site) *[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22301 Two seats of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto/Shrine Historical Sources" * {{Twitter|fuefuki85}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらきいますほのいかつちしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:gōshi]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:葛城市の歴史]] [[Category:葛城市の建築物]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|名かつらきいますほのいかつちしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

seat|WD=Q135018062}} ** P1114: {'amount': '+2', 'unit': '1'} * {{ill|Q135270925|lt=Q135270925|WD=Q135270925}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県葛城市笛吹448', 'language': 'ja'} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Katsuraginimasuhonoikazuchi Jinja Torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Katsuragi-imasuhonoikaduchi-jinja}} [[JA:葛木坐火雷神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11620954}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine |画像=[[File:Fuefuki-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |所在地={{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Katsuragi|Q659672}}Fuefuki 448 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|Honoikazuchi Ōkami|Q10892534}}
{{jalink|Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849}} |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin major}}, 2 seats)
former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建=Unknown (reign of {{jalink|Emperor Jinmu|Q200188}}) |本殿={{jalink|Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545}} |別名=Fuefuki Shrine |例祭={{jalink|October 25|Q2954}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Katsuraki ni Imasu
Honoikaduchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Fuefuki-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine''' (かつらきにいますほのいかづちじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Fuefuki, {{jalink|Katsuragi|Q659672}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and under the former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} system it was a {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. It is commonly called "Fuefuki Shrine." === Enshrined deities === Its principal deities are {{jalink|Honoikazuchi Ōkami|Q10892534}} and {{jalink|Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849}}, with {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Takamimusubi||Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ninigi|Q1056017|Amatsu-hiko Hono-ninigi-no-Mikoto}}, and Ikohitsuhe-no-Mikoto additionally enshrined. The original deity of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine was Honoikazuchi Ōkami, while Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto was the deity of Fuefuki Shrine. Honoikazuchi Ōkami is also called a thunder deity, but here is worshipped as a fire deity. === History === === Overview === The date of foundation is unknown; shrine tradition says either in the age of the gods or in the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Jinmu|Q200188}}. The first documentary appearance is in the ''Buntoku Tennō Jitsuroku'', where in {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} 2 (852), fourth month, it was granted the divine rank of {{jalink|Senior Third Rank|Q11123261}}. According to the {{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}, on the 27th day of the first month of {{jalink|Jōgan|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 1 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), it was granted Junior Second Rank. In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} it is listed as "two seats of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine in Oshiami District, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}," ranked as myojin taisha and receiving offerings for monthly, aidame, and niiname rites. After this, however, references to the shrine disappear. Shrine tradition says the shrine declined in the Heian period and became a {{jalink|subsidiary shrine|Q135009906}} of Fuefuki Shrine in this area. Fuefuki Shrine is thought to have been founded by the Fuefuki no Muraji clan, who were based here. Its deity Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto is their ancestral deity. Behind the honden is an ancient tomb said to be that of {{jalink|Taketahori-no-Mikoto|Q97706292|Taketawake-no-Mikoto}}, father of Kaiko, ancestor of the Fuefuki no Muraji. In {{jalink|1874|Q7761}} (Meiji 7), Honoikazuchi-sha, then a sub-shrine of Fuefuki Shrine, was merged into Fuefuki Shrine, the shrine was renamed Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine, and ranked as gōsha. === Divine rank === * {{jalink|Ten'an|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}) {{jalink|January 27|Q2284}}: from Senior Third Rank, Second Merit Rank to Junior Second Rank, Second Merit Rank ({{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}) - written as "Katsuraki Honoikazuchi deity." === Precincts === There is a Russian-made cannon in the precincts, dedicated by the government after the {{jalink|Russo-Japanese War|Q159950}}. Fuefuki-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden Fuefukijinja Kofun, funkyu.jpg|{{jalink|Fuefuki Shrine Kofun|Q124707325}} behind the honden Fuefuki-jinja, sandou.jpg|Approach in front of haiden Fuefuki-jinja, keidai.jpg|Precincts Fuefuki-jinja, taihou.jpg|Dedicated cannon in the precincts === Rituals === * Otaue Festival ** {{jalink|February 11|Q2335}} ** Early spring festival announcing spring's arrival in Katsuragi. ** After Kinen-sai is held in the haiden, the Otaue rite is performed in the broad court, including mimed rice-planting actions. Then rice cakes are thrown. * Nagoshi Festival ** {{jalink|July 17|Q2729}} ** Parishioners carrying twelve lantern poles visit while singing and accompanying Ise-ondo. The rows of lanterns in the broad court create a fantastic scene. ** Includes dedication performance by the "Katsuragi Taiko Preservation Society." * Chinka Festival ** {{jalink|November 15|Q2982}} ** Following old rites, fire is kindled by friction, giving thanks to fire and praying for fire safety. === Cultural properties === === Nara Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Historic site ** Fuefuki Shrine Kofun - designated March 20, 1998 (Heisei 10). * Natural monument ** Ichiigashi grove of Fuefuki Shrine - designated March 10, 1997 (listed as Shōwa 62 in source). === Access === * 30 minutes west on foot from {{jalink|Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085}} {{jalink|Gose Line}} {{jalink|Oshimi Station|Q7106624}}. === Related items === * {{jalink|Fuefuki Shrine Kofun|Q124707325}} * {{jalink|Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba|Q24862683}} - among fans, this shrine is regarded as one of the pilgrimage "holy sites"[https://jocr.jp/raditopi/2020/08/26/123445/ Is the enshrined deity's name the source of a powerful move name in "Demon Slayer"?! - Interview with the handsome chief priest of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine, Radio Kansai Topics]. === Sources === {{reflist}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Katsuragi-imasuhonoikaduchi-jinja}} *[http://fuefukijinja.org Fuefuki Shrine top page] (official site) *[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22301 Two seats of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto/Shrine Historical Sources" * {{Twitter|fuefuki85}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらきいますほのいかつちしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:gōshi]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:葛城市の歴史]] [[Category:葛城市の建築物]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|名かつらきいますほのいかつちしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: used local AI agents to translate these pages. Contrary to the description it was actually Claude Code for a few, OpenClaw with Codex, and regular Codex. Ollama and DeepSeek were too bad to actually use. see categorization since some of them are very questionable, especially if there was a massive decrease in page size or the {{jawiki| template is minimal or absent on the page

entrance (P9721) == * Katsuraginimasuhonoikazuchi Jinja Torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Katsuragi-imasuhonoikaduchi-jinja}} [[JA:葛木坐火雷神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11620954}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine |画像=[[File:Fuefuki-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |所在地={{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Katsuragi|Q659672}}Fuefuki 448 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|Honoikazuchi Ōkami|Q10892534}}
{{jalink|Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849}} |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin major}}, 2 seats)
former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建=Unknown (reign of {{jalink|Emperor Jinmu|Q200188}}) |本殿={{jalink|Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545}} |別名=Fuefuki Shrine |例祭={{jalink|October 25|Q2954}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Katsuraki ni Imasu
Honoikaduchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Fuefuki-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine''' (かつらきにいますほのいかづちじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Fuefuki, {{jalink|Katsuragi|Q659672}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and under the former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} system it was a {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. It is commonly called "Fuefuki Shrine." === Enshrined deities === Its principal deities are {{jalink|Honoikazuchi Ōkami|Q10892534}} and {{jalink|Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849}}, with {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Takamimusubi||Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ninigi|Q1056017|Amatsu-hiko Hono-ninigi-no-Mikoto}}, and Ikohitsuhe-no-Mikoto additionally enshrined. The original deity of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine was Honoikazuchi Ōkami, while Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto was the deity of Fuefuki Shrine. Honoikazuchi Ōkami is also called a thunder deity, but here is worshipped as a fire deity. === History === === Overview === The date of foundation is unknown; shrine tradition says either in the age of the gods or in the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Jinmu|Q200188}}. The first documentary appearance is in the ''Buntoku Tennō Jitsuroku'', where in {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} 2 (852), fourth month, it was granted the divine rank of {{jalink|Senior Third Rank|Q11123261}}. According to the {{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}, on the 27th day of the first month of {{jalink|Jōgan|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 1 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), it was granted Junior Second Rank. In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} it is listed as "two seats of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine in Oshiami District, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}," ranked as myojin taisha and receiving offerings for monthly, aidame, and niiname rites. After this, however, references to the shrine disappear. Shrine tradition says the shrine declined in the Heian period and became a {{jalink|subsidiary shrine|Q135009906}} of Fuefuki Shrine in this area. Fuefuki Shrine is thought to have been founded by the Fuefuki no Muraji clan, who were based here. Its deity Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto is their ancestral deity. Behind the honden is an ancient tomb said to be that of {{jalink|Taketahori-no-Mikoto|Q97706292|Taketawake-no-Mikoto}}, father of Kaiko, ancestor of the Fuefuki no Muraji. In {{jalink|1874|Q7761}} (Meiji 7), Honoikazuchi-sha, then a sub-shrine of Fuefuki Shrine, was merged into Fuefuki Shrine, the shrine was renamed Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine, and ranked as gōsha. === Divine rank === * {{jalink|Ten'an|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}) {{jalink|January 27|Q2284}}: from Senior Third Rank, Second Merit Rank to Junior Second Rank, Second Merit Rank ({{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}) - written as "Katsuraki Honoikazuchi deity." === Precincts === There is a Russian-made cannon in the precincts, dedicated by the government after the {{jalink|Russo-Japanese War|Q159950}}. Fuefuki-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden Fuefukijinja Kofun, funkyu.jpg|{{jalink|Fuefuki Shrine Kofun|Q124707325}} behind the honden Fuefuki-jinja, sandou.jpg|Approach in front of haiden Fuefuki-jinja, keidai.jpg|Precincts Fuefuki-jinja, taihou.jpg|Dedicated cannon in the precincts === Rituals === * Otaue Festival ** {{jalink|February 11|Q2335}} ** Early spring festival announcing spring's arrival in Katsuragi. ** After Kinen-sai is held in the haiden, the Otaue rite is performed in the broad court, including mimed rice-planting actions. Then rice cakes are thrown. * Nagoshi Festival ** {{jalink|July 17|Q2729}} ** Parishioners carrying twelve lantern poles visit while singing and accompanying Ise-ondo. The rows of lanterns in the broad court create a fantastic scene. ** Includes dedication performance by the "Katsuragi Taiko Preservation Society." * Chinka Festival ** {{jalink|November 15|Q2982}} ** Following old rites, fire is kindled by friction, giving thanks to fire and praying for fire safety. === Cultural properties === === Nara Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Historic site ** Fuefuki Shrine Kofun - designated March 20, 1998 (Heisei 10). * Natural monument ** Ichiigashi grove of Fuefuki Shrine - designated March 10, 1997 (listed as Shōwa 62 in source). === Access === * 30 minutes west on foot from {{jalink|Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085}} {{jalink|Gose Line}} {{jalink|Oshimi Station|Q7106624}}. === Related items === * {{jalink|Fuefuki Shrine Kofun|Q124707325}} * {{jalink|Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba|Q24862683}} - among fans, this shrine is regarded as one of the pilgrimage "holy sites"[https://jocr.jp/raditopi/2020/08/26/123445/ Is the enshrined deity's name the source of a powerful move name in "Demon Slayer"?! - Interview with the handsome chief priest of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine, Radio Kansai Topics]. === Sources === {{reflist}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Katsuragi-imasuhonoikaduchi-jinja}} *[http://fuefukijinja.org Fuefuki Shrine top page] (official site) *[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22301 Two seats of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto/Shrine Historical Sources" * {{Twitter|fuefuki85}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらきいますほのいかつちしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:gōshi]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:葛城市の歴史]] [[Category:葛城市の建築物]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|名かつらきいますほのいかつちしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

entrance (P9721) == * Katsuraginimasuhonoikazuchi Jinja Torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Katsuragi-imasuhonoikaduchi-jinja}} [[JA:葛木坐火雷神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11620954}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine |画像=[[File:Fuefuki-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |所在地={{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Katsuragi|Q659672}}Fuefuki 448 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|Honoikazuchi Ōkami|Q10892534}}
{{jalink|Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849}} |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin major}}, 2 seats)
former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建=Unknown (reign of {{jalink|Emperor Jinmu|Q200188}}) |本殿={{jalink|Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545}} |別名=Fuefuki Shrine |例祭={{jalink|October 25|Q2954}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Katsuraki ni Imasu
Honoikaduchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Fuefuki-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine''' (かつらきにいますほのいかづちじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Fuefuki, {{jalink|Katsuragi|Q659672}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and under the former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} system it was a {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. It is commonly called "Fuefuki Shrine." === Enshrined deities === Its principal deities are {{jalink|Honoikazuchi Ōkami|Q10892534}} and {{jalink|Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849}}, with {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Takamimusubi||Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ninigi|Q1056017|Amatsu-hiko Hono-ninigi-no-Mikoto}}, and Ikohitsuhe-no-Mikoto additionally enshrined. The original deity of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine was Honoikazuchi Ōkami, while Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto was the deity of Fuefuki Shrine. Honoikazuchi Ōkami is also called a thunder deity, but here is worshipped as a fire deity. === History === === Overview === The date of foundation is unknown; shrine tradition says either in the age of the gods or in the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Jinmu|Q200188}}. The first documentary appearance is in the ''Buntoku Tennō Jitsuroku'', where in {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} 2 (852), fourth month, it was granted the divine rank of {{jalink|Senior Third Rank|Q11123261}}. According to the {{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}, on the 27th day of the first month of {{jalink|Jōgan|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 1 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), it was granted Junior Second Rank. In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} it is listed as "two seats of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine in Oshiami District, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}," ranked as myojin taisha and receiving offerings for monthly, aidame, and niiname rites. After this, however, references to the shrine disappear. Shrine tradition says the shrine declined in the Heian period and became a {{jalink|subsidiary shrine|Q135009906}} of Fuefuki Shrine in this area. Fuefuki Shrine is thought to have been founded by the Fuefuki no Muraji clan, who were based here. Its deity Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto is their ancestral deity. Behind the honden is an ancient tomb said to be that of {{jalink|Taketahori-no-Mikoto|Q97706292|Taketawake-no-Mikoto}}, father of Kaiko, ancestor of the Fuefuki no Muraji. In {{jalink|1874|Q7761}} (Meiji 7), Honoikazuchi-sha, then a sub-shrine of Fuefuki Shrine, was merged into Fuefuki Shrine, the shrine was renamed Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine, and ranked as gōsha. === Divine rank === * {{jalink|Ten'an|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}) {{jalink|January 27|Q2284}}: from Senior Third Rank, Second Merit Rank to Junior Second Rank, Second Merit Rank ({{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}) - written as "Katsuraki Honoikazuchi deity." === Precincts === There is a Russian-made cannon in the precincts, dedicated by the government after the {{jalink|Russo-Japanese War|Q159950}}. Fuefuki-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden Fuefukijinja Kofun, funkyu.jpg|{{jalink|Fuefuki Shrine Kofun|Q124707325}} behind the honden Fuefuki-jinja, sandou.jpg|Approach in front of haiden Fuefuki-jinja, keidai.jpg|Precincts Fuefuki-jinja, taihou.jpg|Dedicated cannon in the precincts === Rituals === * Otaue Festival ** {{jalink|February 11|Q2335}} ** Early spring festival announcing spring's arrival in Katsuragi. ** After Kinen-sai is held in the haiden, the Otaue rite is performed in the broad court, including mimed rice-planting actions. Then rice cakes are thrown. * Nagoshi Festival ** {{jalink|July 17|Q2729}} ** Parishioners carrying twelve lantern poles visit while singing and accompanying Ise-ondo. The rows of lanterns in the broad court create a fantastic scene. ** Includes dedication performance by the "Katsuragi Taiko Preservation Society." * Chinka Festival ** {{jalink|November 15|Q2982}} ** Following old rites, fire is kindled by friction, giving thanks to fire and praying for fire safety. === Cultural properties === === Nara Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Historic site ** Fuefuki Shrine Kofun - designated March 20, 1998 (Heisei 10). * Natural monument ** Ichiigashi grove of Fuefuki Shrine - designated March 10, 1997 (listed as Shōwa 62 in source). === Access === * 30 minutes west on foot from {{jalink|Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085}} {{jalink|Gose Line}} {{jalink|Oshimi Station|Q7106624}}. === Related items === * {{jalink|Fuefuki Shrine Kofun|Q124707325}} * {{jalink|Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba|Q24862683}} - among fans, this shrine is regarded as one of the pilgrimage "holy sites"[https://jocr.jp/raditopi/2020/08/26/123445/ Is the enshrined deity's name the source of a powerful move name in "Demon Slayer"?! - Interview with the handsome chief priest of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine, Radio Kansai Topics]. === Sources === {{reflist}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Katsuragi-imasuhonoikaduchi-jinja}} *[http://fuefukijinja.org Fuefuki Shrine top page] (official site) *[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22301 Two seats of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto/Shrine Historical Sources" * {{Twitter|fuefuki85}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらきいますほのいかつちしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:gōshi]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:葛城市の歴史]] [[Category:葛城市の建築物]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|名かつらきいますほのいかつちしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

entrance (P9721) == * Katsuraginimasuhonoikazuchi Jinja Torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Katsuragi-imasuhonoikaduchi-jinja}} [[JA:葛木坐火雷神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11620954}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine |画像=[[File:Fuefuki-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |所在地={{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Katsuragi|Q659672}}Fuefuki 448 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|Honoikazuchi Ōkami|Q10892534}}
{{jalink|Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849}} |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin major}}, 2 seats)
former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建=Unknown (reign of {{jalink|Emperor Jinmu|Q200188}}) |本殿={{jalink|Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545}} |別名=Fuefuki Shrine |例祭={{jalink|October 25|Q2954}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Katsuraki ni Imasu
Honoikaduchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Fuefuki-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine''' (かつらきにいますほのいかづちじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Fuefuki, {{jalink|Katsuragi|Q659672}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and under the former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} system it was a {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. It is commonly called "Fuefuki Shrine." === Enshrined deities === Its principal deities are {{jalink|Honoikazuchi Ōkami|Q10892534}} and {{jalink|Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849}}, with {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Takamimusubi||Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ninigi|Q1056017|Amatsu-hiko Hono-ninigi-no-Mikoto}}, and Ikohitsuhe-no-Mikoto additionally enshrined. The original deity of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine was Honoikazuchi Ōkami, while Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto was the deity of Fuefuki Shrine. Honoikazuchi Ōkami is also called a thunder deity, but here is worshipped as a fire deity. === History === === Overview === The date of foundation is unknown; shrine tradition says either in the age of the gods or in the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Jinmu|Q200188}}. The first documentary appearance is in the ''Buntoku Tennō Jitsuroku'', where in {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} 2 (852), fourth month, it was granted the divine rank of {{jalink|Senior Third Rank|Q11123261}}. According to the {{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}, on the 27th day of the first month of {{jalink|Jōgan|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 1 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), it was granted Junior Second Rank. In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} it is listed as "two seats of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine in Oshiami District, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}," ranked as myojin taisha and receiving offerings for monthly, aidame, and niiname rites. After this, however, references to the shrine disappear. Shrine tradition says the shrine declined in the Heian period and became a {{jalink|subsidiary shrine|Q135009906}} of Fuefuki Shrine in this area. Fuefuki Shrine is thought to have been founded by the Fuefuki no Muraji clan, who were based here. Its deity Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto is their ancestral deity. Behind the honden is an ancient tomb said to be that of {{jalink|Taketahori-no-Mikoto|Q97706292|Taketawake-no-Mikoto}}, father of Kaiko, ancestor of the Fuefuki no Muraji. In {{jalink|1874|Q7761}} (Meiji 7), Honoikazuchi-sha, then a sub-shrine of Fuefuki Shrine, was merged into Fuefuki Shrine, the shrine was renamed Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine, and ranked as gōsha. === Divine rank === * {{jalink|Ten'an|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}) {{jalink|January 27|Q2284}}: from Senior Third Rank, Second Merit Rank to Junior Second Rank, Second Merit Rank ({{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}) - written as "Katsuraki Honoikazuchi deity." === Precincts === There is a Russian-made cannon in the precincts, dedicated by the government after the {{jalink|Russo-Japanese War|Q159950}}. Fuefuki-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden Fuefukijinja Kofun, funkyu.jpg|{{jalink|Fuefuki Shrine Kofun|Q124707325}} behind the honden Fuefuki-jinja, sandou.jpg|Approach in front of haiden Fuefuki-jinja, keidai.jpg|Precincts Fuefuki-jinja, taihou.jpg|Dedicated cannon in the precincts === Rituals === * Otaue Festival ** {{jalink|February 11|Q2335}} ** Early spring festival announcing spring's arrival in Katsuragi. ** After Kinen-sai is held in the haiden, the Otaue rite is performed in the broad court, including mimed rice-planting actions. Then rice cakes are thrown. * Nagoshi Festival ** {{jalink|July 17|Q2729}} ** Parishioners carrying twelve lantern poles visit while singing and accompanying Ise-ondo. The rows of lanterns in the broad court create a fantastic scene. ** Includes dedication performance by the "Katsuragi Taiko Preservation Society." * Chinka Festival ** {{jalink|November 15|Q2982}} ** Following old rites, fire is kindled by friction, giving thanks to fire and praying for fire safety. === Cultural properties === === Nara Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Historic site ** Fuefuki Shrine Kofun - designated March 20, 1998 (Heisei 10). * Natural monument ** Ichiigashi grove of Fuefuki Shrine - designated March 10, 1997 (listed as Shōwa 62 in source). === Access === * 30 minutes west on foot from {{jalink|Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085}} {{jalink|Gose Line}} {{jalink|Oshimi Station|Q7106624}}. === Related items === * {{jalink|Fuefuki Shrine Kofun|Q124707325}} * {{jalink|Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba|Q24862683}} - among fans, this shrine is regarded as one of the pilgrimage "holy sites"[https://jocr.jp/raditopi/2020/08/26/123445/ Is the enshrined deity's name the source of a powerful move name in "Demon Slayer"?! - Interview with the handsome chief priest of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine, Radio Kansai Topics]. === Sources === {{reflist}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Katsuragi-imasuhonoikaduchi-jinja}} *[http://fuefukijinja.org Fuefuki Shrine top page] (official site) *[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22301 Two seats of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto/Shrine Historical Sources" * {{Twitter|fuefuki85}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらきいますほのいかつちしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:gōshi]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:葛城市の歴史]] [[Category:葛城市の建築物]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|名かつらきいますほのいかつちしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

entrance (P9721) == * Katsuraginimasuhonoikazuchi Jinja Torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Katsuragi-imasuhonoikaduchi-jinja}} [[JA:葛木坐火雷神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11620954}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine |画像=[[File:Fuefuki-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |所在地={{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Katsuragi|Q659672}}Fuefuki 448 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|Honoikazuchi Ōkami|Q10892534}}
{{jalink|Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849}} |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin major}}, 2 seats)
former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建=Unknown (reign of {{jalink|Emperor Jinmu|Q200188}}) |本殿={{jalink|Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545}} |別名=Fuefuki Shrine |例祭={{jalink|October 25|Q2954}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Katsuraki ni Imasu
Honoikaduchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Fuefuki-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine''' (かつらきにいますほのいかづちじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Fuefuki, {{jalink|Katsuragi|Q659672}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and under the former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} system it was a {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. It is commonly called "Fuefuki Shrine." === Enshrined deities === Its principal deities are {{jalink|Honoikazuchi Ōkami|Q10892534}} and {{jalink|Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849}}, with {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Takamimusubi||Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ninigi|Q1056017|Amatsu-hiko Hono-ninigi-no-Mikoto}}, and Ikohitsuhe-no-Mikoto additionally enshrined. The original deity of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine was Honoikazuchi Ōkami, while Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto was the deity of Fuefuki Shrine. Honoikazuchi Ōkami is also called a thunder deity, but here is worshipped as a fire deity. === History === === Overview === The date of foundation is unknown; shrine tradition says either in the age of the gods or in the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Jinmu|Q200188}}. The first documentary appearance is in the ''Buntoku Tennō Jitsuroku'', where in {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} 2 (852), fourth month, it was granted the divine rank of {{jalink|Senior Third Rank|Q11123261}}. According to the {{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}, on the 27th day of the first month of {{jalink|Jōgan|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 1 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), it was granted Junior Second Rank. In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} it is listed as "two seats of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine in Oshiami District, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}," ranked as myojin taisha and receiving offerings for monthly, aidame, and niiname rites. After this, however, references to the shrine disappear. Shrine tradition says the shrine declined in the Heian period and became a {{jalink|subsidiary shrine|Q135009906}} of Fuefuki Shrine in this area. Fuefuki Shrine is thought to have been founded by the Fuefuki no Muraji clan, who were based here. Its deity Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto is their ancestral deity. Behind the honden is an ancient tomb said to be that of {{jalink|Taketahori-no-Mikoto|Q97706292|Taketawake-no-Mikoto}}, father of Kaiko, ancestor of the Fuefuki no Muraji. In {{jalink|1874|Q7761}} (Meiji 7), Honoikazuchi-sha, then a sub-shrine of Fuefuki Shrine, was merged into Fuefuki Shrine, the shrine was renamed Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine, and ranked as gōsha. === Divine rank === * {{jalink|Ten'an|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}) {{jalink|January 27|Q2284}}: from Senior Third Rank, Second Merit Rank to Junior Second Rank, Second Merit Rank ({{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}) - written as "Katsuraki Honoikazuchi deity." === Precincts === There is a Russian-made cannon in the precincts, dedicated by the government after the {{jalink|Russo-Japanese War|Q159950}}. Fuefuki-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden Fuefukijinja Kofun, funkyu.jpg|{{jalink|Fuefuki Shrine Kofun|Q124707325}} behind the honden Fuefuki-jinja, sandou.jpg|Approach in front of haiden Fuefuki-jinja, keidai.jpg|Precincts Fuefuki-jinja, taihou.jpg|Dedicated cannon in the precincts === Rituals === * Otaue Festival ** {{jalink|February 11|Q2335}} ** Early spring festival announcing spring's arrival in Katsuragi. ** After Kinen-sai is held in the haiden, the Otaue rite is performed in the broad court, including mimed rice-planting actions. Then rice cakes are thrown. * Nagoshi Festival ** {{jalink|July 17|Q2729}} ** Parishioners carrying twelve lantern poles visit while singing and accompanying Ise-ondo. The rows of lanterns in the broad court create a fantastic scene. ** Includes dedication performance by the "Katsuragi Taiko Preservation Society." * Chinka Festival ** {{jalink|November 15|Q2982}} ** Following old rites, fire is kindled by friction, giving thanks to fire and praying for fire safety. === Cultural properties === === Nara Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Historic site ** Fuefuki Shrine Kofun - designated March 20, 1998 (Heisei 10). * Natural monument ** Ichiigashi grove of Fuefuki Shrine - designated March 10, 1997 (listed as Shōwa 62 in source). === Access === * 30 minutes west on foot from {{jalink|Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085}} {{jalink|Gose Line}} {{jalink|Oshimi Station|Q7106624}}. === Related items === * {{jalink|Fuefuki Shrine Kofun|Q124707325}} * {{jalink|Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba|Q24862683}} - among fans, this shrine is regarded as one of the pilgrimage "holy sites"[https://jocr.jp/raditopi/2020/08/26/123445/ Is the enshrined deity's name the source of a powerful move name in "Demon Slayer"?! - Interview with the handsome chief priest of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine, Radio Kansai Topics]. === Sources === {{reflist}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Katsuragi-imasuhonoikaduchi-jinja}} *[http://fuefukijinja.org Fuefuki Shrine top page] (official site) *[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22301 Two seats of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto/Shrine Historical Sources" * {{Twitter|fuefuki85}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらきいますほのいかつちしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:gōshi]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:葛城市の歴史]] [[Category:葛城市の建築物]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|名かつらきいますほのいかつちしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:pages]]

entrance (P9721) == * Katsuraginimasuhonoikazuchi Jinja Torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Katsuragi-imasuhonoikaduchi-jinja}} [[JA:葛木坐火雷神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11620954}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine |画像=[[File:Fuefuki-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |所在地={{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Katsuragi|Q659672}}Fuefuki 448 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|Honoikazuchi Ōkami|Q10892534}}
{{jalink|Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849}} |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin major}}, 2 seats)
former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建=Unknown (reign of {{jalink|Emperor Jinmu|Q200188}}) |本殿={{jalink|Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545}} |別名=Fuefuki Shrine |例祭={{jalink|October 25|Q2954}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Katsuraki ni Imasu
Honoikaduchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Fuefuki-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine''' (かつらきにいますほのいかづちじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Fuefuki, {{jalink|Katsuragi|Q659672}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and under the former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} system it was a {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. It is commonly called "Fuefuki Shrine." === Enshrined deities === Its principal deities are {{jalink|Honoikazuchi Ōkami|Q10892534}} and {{jalink|Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849}}, with {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Takamimusubi||Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ninigi|Q1056017|Amatsu-hiko Hono-ninigi-no-Mikoto}}, and Ikohitsuhe-no-Mikoto additionally enshrined. The original deity of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine was Honoikazuchi Ōkami, while Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto was the deity of Fuefuki Shrine. Honoikazuchi Ōkami is also called a thunder deity, but here is worshipped as a fire deity. === History === === Overview === The date of foundation is unknown; shrine tradition says either in the age of the gods or in the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Jinmu|Q200188}}. The first documentary appearance is in the ''Buntoku Tennō Jitsuroku'', where in {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} 2 (852), fourth month, it was granted the divine rank of {{jalink|Senior Third Rank|Q11123261}}. According to the {{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}, on the 27th day of the first month of {{jalink|Jōgan|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 1 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), it was granted Junior Second Rank. In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} it is listed as "two seats of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine in Oshiami District, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}," ranked as myojin taisha and receiving offerings for monthly, aidame, and niiname rites. After this, however, references to the shrine disappear. Shrine tradition says the shrine declined in the Heian period and became a {{jalink|subsidiary shrine|Q135009906}} of Fuefuki Shrine in this area. Fuefuki Shrine is thought to have been founded by the Fuefuki no Muraji clan, who were based here. Its deity Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto is their ancestral deity. Behind the honden is an ancient tomb said to be that of {{jalink|Taketahori-no-Mikoto|Q97706292|Taketawake-no-Mikoto}}, father of Kaiko, ancestor of the Fuefuki no Muraji. In {{jalink|1874|Q7761}} (Meiji 7), Honoikazuchi-sha, then a sub-shrine of Fuefuki Shrine, was merged into Fuefuki Shrine, the shrine was renamed Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine, and ranked as gōsha. === Divine rank === * {{jalink|Ten'an|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}) {{jalink|January 27|Q2284}}: from Senior Third Rank, Second Merit Rank to Junior Second Rank, Second Merit Rank ({{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}) - written as "Katsuraki Honoikazuchi deity." === Precincts === There is a Russian-made cannon in the precincts, dedicated by the government after the {{jalink|Russo-Japanese War|Q159950}}. Fuefuki-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden Fuefukijinja Kofun, funkyu.jpg|{{jalink|Fuefuki Shrine Kofun|Q124707325}} behind the honden Fuefuki-jinja, sandou.jpg|Approach in front of haiden Fuefuki-jinja, keidai.jpg|Precincts Fuefuki-jinja, taihou.jpg|Dedicated cannon in the precincts === Rituals === * Otaue Festival ** {{jalink|February 11|Q2335}} ** Early spring festival announcing spring's arrival in Katsuragi. ** After Kinen-sai is held in the haiden, the Otaue rite is performed in the broad court, including mimed rice-planting actions. Then rice cakes are thrown. * Nagoshi Festival ** {{jalink|July 17|Q2729}} ** Parishioners carrying twelve lantern poles visit while singing and accompanying Ise-ondo. The rows of lanterns in the broad court create a fantastic scene. ** Includes dedication performance by the "Katsuragi Taiko Preservation Society." * Chinka Festival ** {{jalink|November 15|Q2982}} ** Following old rites, fire is kindled by friction, giving thanks to fire and praying for fire safety. === Cultural properties === === Nara Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Historic site ** Fuefuki Shrine Kofun - designated March 20, 1998 (Heisei 10). * Natural monument ** Ichiigashi grove of Fuefuki Shrine - designated March 10, 1997 (listed as Shōwa 62 in source). === Access === * 30 minutes west on foot from {{jalink|Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085}} {{jalink|Gose Line}} {{jalink|Oshimi Station|Q7106624}}. === Related items === * {{jalink|Fuefuki Shrine Kofun|Q124707325}} * {{jalink|Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba|Q24862683}} - among fans, this shrine is regarded as one of the pilgrimage "holy sites"[https://jocr.jp/raditopi/2020/08/26/123445/ Is the enshrined deity's name the source of a powerful move name in "Demon Slayer"?! - Interview with the handsome chief priest of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine, Radio Kansai Topics]. === Sources === {{reflist}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Katsuragi-imasuhonoikaduchi-jinja}} *[http://fuefukijinja.org Fuefuki Shrine top page] (official site) *[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22301 Two seats of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto/Shrine Historical Sources" * {{Twitter|fuefuki85}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらきいますほのいかつちしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:gōshi]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:葛城市の歴史]] [[Category:葛城市の建築物]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|名かつらきいますほのいかつちしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]]

entrance (P9721) == * Katsuraginimasuhonoikazuchi Jinja Torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Katsuragi-imasuhonoikaduchi-jinja}} [[JA:葛木坐火雷神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11620954}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine |画像=[[File:Fuefuki-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |所在地={{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Katsuragi|Q659672}}Fuefuki 448 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|Honoikazuchi Ōkami|Q10892534}}
{{jalink|Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849}} |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin major}}, 2 seats)
former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建=Unknown (reign of {{jalink|Emperor Jinmu|Q200188}}) |本殿={{jalink|Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545}} |別名=Fuefuki Shrine |例祭={{jalink|October 25|Q2954}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Katsuraki ni Imasu
Honoikaduchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Fuefuki-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine''' (かつらきにいますほのいかづちじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Fuefuki, {{jalink|Katsuragi|Q659672}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and under the former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} system it was a {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. It is commonly called "Fuefuki Shrine." === Enshrined deities === Its principal deities are {{jalink|Honoikazuchi Ōkami|Q10892534}} and {{jalink|Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849}}, with {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Takamimusubi||Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ninigi|Q1056017|Amatsu-hiko Hono-ninigi-no-Mikoto}}, and Ikohitsuhe-no-Mikoto additionally enshrined. The original deity of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine was Honoikazuchi Ōkami, while Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto was the deity of Fuefuki Shrine. Honoikazuchi Ōkami is also called a thunder deity, but here is worshipped as a fire deity. === History === === Overview === The date of foundation is unknown; shrine tradition says either in the age of the gods or in the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Jinmu|Q200188}}. The first documentary appearance is in the ''Buntoku Tennō Jitsuroku'', where in {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} 2 (852), fourth month, it was granted the divine rank of {{jalink|Senior Third Rank|Q11123261}}. According to the {{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}, on the 27th day of the first month of {{jalink|Jōgan|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 1 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), it was granted Junior Second Rank. In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} it is listed as "two seats of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine in Oshiami District, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}," ranked as myojin taisha and receiving offerings for monthly, aidame, and niiname rites. After this, however, references to the shrine disappear. Shrine tradition says the shrine declined in the Heian period and became a {{jalink|subsidiary shrine|Q135009906}} of Fuefuki Shrine in this area. Fuefuki Shrine is thought to have been founded by the Fuefuki no Muraji clan, who were based here. Its deity Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto is their ancestral deity. Behind the honden is an ancient tomb said to be that of {{jalink|Taketahori-no-Mikoto|Q97706292|Taketawake-no-Mikoto}}, father of Kaiko, ancestor of the Fuefuki no Muraji. In {{jalink|1874|Q7761}} (Meiji 7), Honoikazuchi-sha, then a sub-shrine of Fuefuki Shrine, was merged into Fuefuki Shrine, the shrine was renamed Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine, and ranked as gōsha. === Divine rank === * {{jalink|Ten'an|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}) {{jalink|January 27|Q2284}}: from Senior Third Rank, Second Merit Rank to Junior Second Rank, Second Merit Rank ({{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}) - written as "Katsuraki Honoikazuchi deity." === Precincts === There is a Russian-made cannon in the precincts, dedicated by the government after the {{jalink|Russo-Japanese War|Q159950}}. Fuefuki-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden Fuefukijinja Kofun, funkyu.jpg|{{jalink|Fuefuki Shrine Kofun|Q124707325}} behind the honden Fuefuki-jinja, sandou.jpg|Approach in front of haiden Fuefuki-jinja, keidai.jpg|Precincts Fuefuki-jinja, taihou.jpg|Dedicated cannon in the precincts === Rituals === * Otaue Festival ** {{jalink|February 11|Q2335}} ** Early spring festival announcing spring's arrival in Katsuragi. ** After Kinen-sai is held in the haiden, the Otaue rite is performed in the broad court, including mimed rice-planting actions. Then rice cakes are thrown. * Nagoshi Festival ** {{jalink|July 17|Q2729}} ** Parishioners carrying twelve lantern poles visit while singing and accompanying Ise-ondo. The rows of lanterns in the broad court create a fantastic scene. ** Includes dedication performance by the "Katsuragi Taiko Preservation Society." * Chinka Festival ** {{jalink|November 15|Q2982}} ** Following old rites, fire is kindled by friction, giving thanks to fire and praying for fire safety. === Cultural properties === === Nara Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Historic site ** Fuefuki Shrine Kofun - designated March 20, 1998 (Heisei 10). * Natural monument ** Ichiigashi grove of Fuefuki Shrine - designated March 10, 1997 (listed as Shōwa 62 in source). === Access === * 30 minutes west on foot from {{jalink|Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085}} {{jalink|Gose Line}} {{jalink|Oshimi Station|Q7106624}}. === Related items === * {{jalink|Fuefuki Shrine Kofun|Q124707325}} * {{jalink|Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba|Q24862683}} - among fans, this shrine is regarded as one of the pilgrimage "holy sites"[https://jocr.jp/raditopi/2020/08/26/123445/ Is the enshrined deity's name the source of a powerful move name in "Demon Slayer"?! - Interview with the handsome chief priest of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine, Radio Kansai Topics]. === Sources === {{reflist}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Katsuragi-imasuhonoikaduchi-jinja}} *[http://fuefukijinja.org Fuefuki Shrine top page] (official site) *[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22301 Two seats of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto/Shrine Historical Sources" * {{Twitter|fuefuki85}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらきいますほのいかつちしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:gōshi]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:葛城市の歴史]] [[Category:葛城市の建築物]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|名かつらきいますほのいかつちしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

entrance (P9721) == * Katsuraginimasuhonoikazuchi Jinja Torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Katsuragi-imasuhonoikaduchi-jinja}} [[JA:葛木坐火雷神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11620954}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine |画像=[[File:Fuefuki-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |所在地={{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Katsuragi|Q659672}}Fuefuki 448 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|Honoikazuchi Ōkami|Q10892534}}
{{jalink|Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849}} |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin major}}, 2 seats)
former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建=Unknown (reign of {{jalink|Emperor Jinmu|Q200188}}) |本殿={{jalink|Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545}} |別名=Fuefuki Shrine |例祭={{jalink|October 25|Q2954}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Katsuraki ni Imasu
Honoikaduchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Fuefuki-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine''' (かつらきにいますほのいかづちじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Fuefuki, {{jalink|Katsuragi|Q659672}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and under the former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} system it was a {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. It is commonly called "Fuefuki Shrine." === Enshrined deities === Its principal deities are {{jalink|Honoikazuchi Ōkami|Q10892534}} and {{jalink|Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849}}, with {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Takamimusubi||Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ninigi|Q1056017|Amatsu-hiko Hono-ninigi-no-Mikoto}}, and Ikohitsuhe-no-Mikoto additionally enshrined. The original deity of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine was Honoikazuchi Ōkami, while Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto was the deity of Fuefuki Shrine. Honoikazuchi Ōkami is also called a thunder deity, but here is worshipped as a fire deity. === History === === Overview === The date of foundation is unknown; shrine tradition says either in the age of the gods or in the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Jinmu|Q200188}}. The first documentary appearance is in the ''Buntoku Tennō Jitsuroku'', where in {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} 2 (852), fourth month, it was granted the divine rank of {{jalink|Senior Third Rank|Q11123261}}. According to the {{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}, on the 27th day of the first month of {{jalink|Jōgan|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 1 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), it was granted Junior Second Rank. In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} it is listed as "two seats of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine in Oshiami District, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}}," ranked as myojin taisha and receiving offerings for monthly, aidame, and niiname rites. After this, however, references to the shrine disappear. Shrine tradition says the shrine declined in the Heian period and became a {{jalink|subsidiary shrine|Q135009906}} of Fuefuki Shrine in this area. Fuefuki Shrine is thought to have been founded by the Fuefuki no Muraji clan, who were based here. Its deity Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto is their ancestral deity. Behind the honden is an ancient tomb said to be that of {{jalink|Taketahori-no-Mikoto|Q97706292|Taketawake-no-Mikoto}}, father of Kaiko, ancestor of the Fuefuki no Muraji. In {{jalink|1874|Q7761}} (Meiji 7), Honoikazuchi-sha, then a sub-shrine of Fuefuki Shrine, was merged into Fuefuki Shrine, the shrine was renamed Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine, and ranked as gōsha. === Divine rank === * {{jalink|Ten'an|Q1075611|Ten'an}} 3 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}) {{jalink|January 27|Q2284}}: from Senior Third Rank, Second Merit Rank to Junior Second Rank, Second Merit Rank ({{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}) - written as "Katsuraki Honoikazuchi deity." === Precincts === There is a Russian-made cannon in the precincts, dedicated by the government after the {{jalink|Russo-Japanese War|Q159950}}. Fuefuki-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden Fuefukijinja Kofun, funkyu.jpg|{{jalink|Fuefuki Shrine Kofun|Q124707325}} behind the honden Fuefuki-jinja, sandou.jpg|Approach in front of haiden Fuefuki-jinja, keidai.jpg|Precincts Fuefuki-jinja, taihou.jpg|Dedicated cannon in the precincts === Rituals === * Otaue Festival ** {{jalink|February 11|Q2335}} ** Early spring festival announcing spring's arrival in Katsuragi. ** After Kinen-sai is held in the haiden, the Otaue rite is performed in the broad court, including mimed rice-planting actions. Then rice cakes are thrown. * Nagoshi Festival ** {{jalink|July 17|Q2729}} ** Parishioners carrying twelve lantern poles visit while singing and accompanying Ise-ondo. The rows of lanterns in the broad court create a fantastic scene. ** Includes dedication performance by the "Katsuragi Taiko Preservation Society." * Chinka Festival ** {{jalink|November 15|Q2982}} ** Following old rites, fire is kindled by friction, giving thanks to fire and praying for fire safety. === Cultural properties === === Nara Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Historic site ** Fuefuki Shrine Kofun - designated March 20, 1998 (Heisei 10). * Natural monument ** Ichiigashi grove of Fuefuki Shrine - designated March 10, 1997 (listed as Shōwa 62 in source). === Access === * 30 minutes west on foot from {{jalink|Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085}} {{jalink|Gose Line}} {{jalink|Oshimi Station|Q7106624}}. === Related items === * {{jalink|Fuefuki Shrine Kofun|Q124707325}} * {{jalink|Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba|Q24862683}} - among fans, this shrine is regarded as one of the pilgrimage "holy sites"[https://jocr.jp/raditopi/2020/08/26/123445/ Is the enshrined deity's name the source of a powerful move name in "Demon Slayer"?! - Interview with the handsome chief priest of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine, Radio Kansai Topics]. === Sources === {{reflist}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Katsuragi-imasuhonoikaduchi-jinja}} *[http://fuefukijinja.org Fuefuki Shrine top page] (official site) *[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22301 Two seats of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto/Shrine Historical Sources" * {{Twitter|fuefuki85}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらきいますほのいかつちしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:gōshi]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:葛城市の歴史]] [[Category:葛城市の建築物]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|名かつらきいますほのいかつちしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

entrance (P9721) == * Katsuraginimasuhonoikazuchi Jinja Torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Katsuragi-imasuhonoikaduchi-jinja}} [[JA:葛木坐火雷神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11620954}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine |画像=[[File:Fuefuki-jinja, haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Katsuragi|Q659672|WD=Q659672}}Fuefuki 448 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Honoikazuchi Ōkami|Q10892534|WD=Q10892534}}
{{ill|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849}} |社格={{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin major}}, 2 seats)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建=Unknown (reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}}) |本殿={{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545}} |別名=Fuefuki Shrine |例祭={{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Katsuraki ni Imasu
Honoikaduchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Fuefuki-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine''' (かつらきにいますほのいかづちじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Fuefuki, {{ill|lt=Katsuragi|Q659672|WD=Q659672}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}), and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It is commonly called "Fuefuki Shrine." === Enshrined deities === Its principal deities are {{ill|lt=Honoikazuchi Ōkami|Q10892534|WD=Q10892534}} and {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto|Q10940849|WD=Q10940849}}, with {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Takamimusubi||WD=|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Amatsu-hiko Hono-ninigi-no-Mikoto}}, and Ikohitsuhe-no-Mikoto additionally enshrined. The original deity of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine was Honoikazuchi Ōkami, while Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto was the deity of Fuefuki Shrine. Honoikazuchi Ōkami is also called a thunder deity, but here is worshipped as a fire deity. === History === === Overview === The date of foundation is unknown; shrine tradition says either in the age of the gods or in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}}. The first documentary appearance is in the ''Buntoku Tennō Jitsuroku'', where in {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077}} 2 (852), fourth month, it was granted the divine rank of {{ill|lt=Senior Third Rank|Q11123261|WD=Q11123261}}. According to the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}, on the 27th day of the first month of {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), it was granted Junior Second Rank. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} it is listed as "two seats of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine in Oshiami District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}," ranked as myojin taisha and receiving offerings for monthly, aidame, and niiname rites. After this, however, references to the shrine disappear. Shrine tradition says the shrine declined in the Heian period and became a {{ill|lt=subsidiary shrine|Q135009906|WD=Q135009906}} of Fuefuki Shrine in this area. Fuefuki Shrine is thought to have been founded by the Fuefuki no Muraji clan, who were based here. Its deity Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto is their ancestral deity. Behind the honden is an ancient tomb said to be that of {{ill|lt=Taketahori-no-Mikoto|Q97706292|WD=Q97706292|lt=Taketawake-no-Mikoto}}, father of Kaiko, ancestor of the Fuefuki no Muraji. In {{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761}} (Meiji 7), Honoikazuchi-sha, then a sub-shrine of Fuefuki Shrine, was merged into Fuefuki Shrine, the shrine was renamed Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine, and ranked as gōsha. === Divine rank === * {{ill|lt=Ten'an|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}) {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284}}: from Senior Third Rank, Second Merit Rank to Junior Second Rank, Second Merit Rank ({{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}) - written as "Katsuraki Honoikazuchi deity." === Precincts === There is a Russian-made cannon in the precincts, dedicated by the government after the {{ill|lt=Russo-Japanese War|Q159950|WD=Q159950}}. Fuefuki-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden Fuefukijinja Kofun, funkyu.jpg|{{ill|lt=Fuefuki Shrine Kofun|Q124707325|WD=Q124707325}} behind the honden Fuefuki-jinja, sandou.jpg|Approach in front of haiden Fuefuki-jinja, keidai.jpg|Precincts Fuefuki-jinja, taihou.jpg|Dedicated cannon in the precincts === Rituals === * Otaue Festival ** {{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|WD=Q2335}} ** Early spring festival announcing spring's arrival in Katsuragi. ** After Kinen-sai is held in the haiden, the Otaue rite is performed in the broad court, including mimed rice-planting actions. Then rice cakes are thrown. * Nagoshi Festival ** {{ill|lt=July 17|Q2729|WD=Q2729}} ** Parishioners carrying twelve lantern poles visit while singing and accompanying Ise-ondo. The rows of lanterns in the broad court create a fantastic scene. ** Includes dedication performance by the "Katsuragi Taiko Preservation Society." * Chinka Festival ** {{ill|lt=November 15|Q2982|WD=Q2982}} ** Following old rites, fire is kindled by friction, giving thanks to fire and praying for fire safety. === Cultural properties === === Nara Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Historic site ** Fuefuki Shrine Kofun - designated March 20, 1998 (Heisei 10). * Natural monument ** Ichiigashi grove of Fuefuki Shrine - designated March 10, 1997 (listed as Shōwa 62 in source). === Access === * 30 minutes west on foot from {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085}} {{ill||lt=Gose Line|UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Oshimi Station|Q7106624|WD=Q7106624}}. === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Fuefuki Shrine Kofun|Q124707325|WD=Q124707325}} * {{ill|lt=Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba|Q24862683|WD=Q24862683}} - among fans, this shrine is regarded as one of the pilgrimage "holy sites"[https://jocr.jp/raditopi/2020/08/26/123445/ Is the enshrined deity's name the source of a powerful move name in "Demon Slayer"?! - Interview with the handsome chief priest of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine, Radio Kansai Topics]. === Sources === {{reflist}} === External links === {{commons|Category:Katsuragi-imasuhonoikaduchi-jinja}} *[http://fuefukijinja.org Fuefuki Shrine top page] (official site) *[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22301 Two seats of Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Compilation of Shinto/Shrine Historical Sources" * {{Twitter|fuefuki85}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらきいますほのいかつちしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsuraki ni Imasu Honoikaduchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:gōshi]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:葛城市の歴史]] [[Category:葛城市の建築物]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|名かつらきいますほのいかつちしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]
